challenges.title: Two Sum
challenges.description:
Given an array of integers `nums` and an integer `target`, return indices of the two numbers such that they add up to target.

You may assume that each input would have exactly one solution, and you may not use the same element twice.

You can return the answer in any order.
hints: A really brute force way would be to search for all possible pairs of numbers but that would be too slow. Again, it's best to try out brute force solutions for just for completeness. It is from these brute force solutions that you can come up with optimizations.

So, if we fix one of the numbers, say x, we have to scan the entire array to find the next number y which is value - x where value is the input parameter. Can we change our array somehow so that this search becomes faster?
The second train of thought is, without changing the array, can we use additional space somehow? Like maybe a hash map to speed up the search?

challenges.title: Valid Palindrome
challenges.description:
A phrase is a palindrome if, after converting all uppercase letters into lowercase letters and removing all non-alphanumeric characters, it reads the same forward and backward. Alphanumeric characters include letters and numbers.

Given a string `s`, return `true` if it is a palindrome, or `false` otherwise.

examples.input: s = "A man, a plan, a canal: Panama"

examples.explanation: s is an empty string "" after removing non-alphanumeric characters. Since an empty string reads the same forward and backward, it is a palindrome.

constraints: s consists only of printable ASCII characters.

input.s: A man, a plan, a canal: Panama

hints: Can you solve this problem using the two-pointer technique?
Consider what characters you need to ignore and how to handle case sensitivity.
Think about how to efficiently check if the cleaned string is a palindrome.

defaultChallenge.title: Two Sum
defaultChallenge.description:
Given an array of integers `nums` and an integer `target`, return indices of the two numbers such that they add up to target.

You may assume that each input would have exactly one solution, and you may not use the same element twice.

You can return the answer in any order.
hints: Try using a hash map to store numbers you've seen and their indices.
For each number, check if target - number exists in your hash map.
